subscription.provider: Codex / OpenAI + Gemini + Grok OAuth / xAI
subscription.note: Subscription-backed usage is capacity consumption, not incremental API spend.

providerBudgets.label: Codex / OpenAI
providerBudgets.lastRouteReason: Default for repo edits, private connectors, auth, terminal execution, approvals, and final integration.
providerBudgets.whyChosen: Default for repo edits, private connectors, auth, terminal execution, approvals, and final integration.

providerBudgets.label: Gemini
providerBudgets.role: JAIMES-first reviewer, evaluator, digest, and long-context synthesis lane
providerBudgets.lastRouteReason: Use Gemini Pro first for JAIMES dashboard-safe summaries, digests, reports, log summaries, reviews, and stale-task compression; use Flash only as fallback/cheap throughput; never raw secrets, OAuth payloads, raw mail, or private account data.
providerBudgets.whyChosen: Use Gemini Pro first for JAIMES dashboard-safe summaries, digests, reports, log summaries, reviews, and stale-task compression; use Flash only as fallback/cheap throughput; never raw secrets, OAuth payloads, raw mail, or private account data.

providerBudgets.label: Grok OAuth / xAI
providerBudgets.role: X/SuperGrok subscription CLI lane plus xAI API feed for X-native/current-events research
providerBudgets.lastRouteReason: Use xAI API-backed x_search/Grok reasoning for X/current-events feed; use Grok CLI OAuth for subscription/account-backed CLI tasks. Keep Grok specialist, not default.
providerBudgets.feeNote: Monthly fee is an estimated FinOps placeholder; exact X billing is not available to CLI.
providerBudgets.probeCalibrationNote: 50 calls consumed 2% per Josh screenshot; linear estimate only.
providerBudgets.whyChosen: Use xAI API-backed x_search/Grok reasoning for X/current-events feed; use Grok CLI OAuth for subscription/account-backed CLI tasks. Keep Grok specialist, not default.

providerBudgets.label: OpenRouter
providerBudgets.role: Fallback route when primary lanes are unavailable or a specific outside model is needed
providerBudgets.lastRouteReason: Fallback only; prefer Codex, Gemini, or xAI specialist lanes first.
providerBudgets.whyChosen: Fallback only; prefer Codex, Gemini, or xAI specialist lanes first.

codexExhaustedMode.trigger: Set codexAllowanceMode to exhausted when Codex weekly allowance is gone or Josh explicitly requests outage continuity.
codexExhaustedMode.rule: Route dashboard-safe summaries, reviews, digests, stale-task compression, and non-execution synthesis to Gemini. Route public current-events, X-native research, outside critique, and batch audits to xAI. Route explicit non-sensitive fallback checks to OpenRouter. Reserve Codex, OpenAI API, or OpenRouter spend for execution, private connector work, repo edits, terminal actions, approvals, and final integration.
codexExhaustedMode.telegramDisclosure: Josh 2.0 and JAIMES must state Active Model/Auth and Route reason before doing substantive work while this mode is active.

routerPolicy.rule: Codex/OpenAI remains default execution. JAIMES should use Gemini Pro first for dashboard-safe review, digest, reports, stale-task compression, summaries, and long-context evaluation when sufficient. Use Gemini Flash only as cheap fallback or explicitly lightweight/noisy throughput. xAI handles X-native/current-events specialist work. OpenRouter is fallback only.
routerPolicy.lastModeChangeReason: make Gemini Pro the default JAIMES-safe synthesis lane

codexExhaustedMode.trigger: Set codexAllowanceMode to exhausted when Codex weekly allowance is gone or Josh explicitly requests outage continuity.
codexExhaustedMode.rule: Route dashboard-safe summaries, reviews, digests, stale-task compression, and non-execution synthesis to Gemini. Route public current-events, X-native research, outside critique, and batch audits to xAI. Route explicit non-sensitive fallback checks to OpenRouter. Reserve Codex, OpenAI API, or OpenRouter spend for execution, private connector work, repo edits, terminal actions, approvals, and final integration.
codexExhaustedMode.telegramDisclosure: Josh 2.0 and JAIMES must state Active Model/Auth and Route reason before doing substantive work while this mode is active.

policy.rule: Codex/OpenAI remains default execution. Use Gemini Flash only as cheap fallback or explicitly lightweight/noisy throughput. xAI handles X-native/current-events specialist work. OpenRouter is fallback only.
policy.lastModeChangeReason: make Gemini Pro the default JAIMES-safe synthesis lane

providers.label: Codex / OpenAI
providers.lastRouteReason: Default for repo edits, private connectors, auth, terminal execution, approvals, and final integration.
providers.whyChosen: Default for repo edits, private connectors, auth, terminal execution, approvals, and final integration.

providers.label: Gemini
providers.role: JAIMES-first reviewer, evaluator, digest, and long-context synthesis lane
providers.lastRouteReason: Use Gemini Pro first for JAIMES dashboard-safe summaries, digests, reports, log summaries, reviews, and stale-task compression; use Flash only as fallback/cheap throughput; never raw secrets, OAuth payloads, raw mail, or private account data.
providers.whyChosen: Use Gemini Pro first for JAIMES dashboard-safe summaries, digests, reports, log summaries, reviews, and stale-task compression; use Flash only as fallback/cheap throughput; never raw secrets, OAuth payloads, raw mail, or private account data.

providers.label: Grok OAuth / xAI
providers.role: X/SuperGrok subscription CLI lane plus xAI API feed for X-native/current-events research
providers.lastRouteReason: Use xAI API-backed x_search/Grok reasoning for X/current-events feed; use Grok CLI OAuth for subscription/account-backed CLI tasks. Keep Grok specialist, not default.
providers.feeNote: Monthly fee is an estimated FinOps placeholder; exact X billing is not available to CLI.
providers.probeCalibrationNote: 50 calls consumed 2% per Josh screenshot; linear estimate only.
providers.whyChosen: Use xAI API-backed x_search/Grok reasoning for X/current-events feed; use Grok CLI OAuth for subscription/account-backed CLI tasks. Keep Grok specialist, not default.

providers.label: OpenRouter
providers.role: Fallback route when primary lanes are unavailable or a specific outside model is needed
providers.lastRouteReason: Fallback only; prefer Codex, Gemini, or xAI specialist lanes first.
providers.whyChosen: Fallback only; prefer Codex, Gemini, or xAI specialist lanes first.

guardrails: Do not route secrets, OAuth payloads, raw connector data, raw emails, or private account contents to Gemini, xAI, or OpenRouter automatically.
Any provider with a daily cap is blocked once dailySpendUsd is greater than or equal to dailyCapUsd unless Josh explicitly approves an override.
When Codex allowance mode is exhausted, non-execution dashboard-safe work must leave Codex first; Codex/API/OpenRouter spend is reserved for execution and private actions.
xAI is not the default brain. It is a specialist lane for current-events, X-native research, outside critique, and batch audits.
modelRouter.summary: Codex default; Gemini reviewer; xAI current-events/X-native specialist; OpenRouter fallback.

agents.label: JOSHeX / Personal Codex
responsibilities: Mission Control and Agent Control code changes
Gemini CLI brokering for approved dashboard-safe specialist tasks

deviceResourcePolicy.summary: JOSHeX is the trusted coordinator and private-Mac specialist, not the default execution host. Prefer Josh 2.0 or JAIMES for real ecosystem work unless the task requires MacBook-only local state.
offloadToJosh2: browser automation that should not disturb the MacBook

codexExhaustedMode.activation: Set model-provider-budgets.json policy.codexAllowanceMode to exhausted, or call scripts/agent_route.py with --codex-allowance exhausted.
codexExhaustedMode.geminiFirst: dashboard-safe summaries, reviews, digests, reports, handoffs, runbooks, stale-task compression, and non-execution synthesis
codexExhaustedMode.xaiFirst: public current-events, X-native research, outside critique, red-team review, and batch/config audits
codexExhaustedMode.openRouterFallback: explicit non-sensitive provider fallback checks or outside-model comparisons
codexExhaustedMode.codexReserved: repo edits, terminal actions, tests, deployment, authenticated browser work, private connectors, secrets, approvals, account mutation, and final integration

providerRoles.codex: OpenAI Codex OAuth/subscription: default for execution, code edits, repo work, shell/tools, Control Tower/OpenCLAW, auth-sensitive workflows, and final verification.
providerRoles.gemini: Gemini CLI OAuth/subscription: default for dashboard-safe reading, summarizing, comparison, second-pass review, report cleanup, and research digestion.
providerRoles.xai: Grok/xAI specialist: X-native, current-events/social signal, creator posts, crypto/news pulse, public reaction, and zeitgeist checks.
providerRoles.openrouter: Fallback/specialist route only; not primary spend.

modelRouting.guardrail: Codex/OpenAI remains default execution model, but JOSHeX on the personal MacBook is not the default execution host. Prefer Josh 2.0 or JAIMES for ecosystem execution and heavier work. Gemini, xAI, and OpenRouter receive sanitized dashboard-safe specialist work only. xAI calls should use store=false where available and must not receive secrets or raw private connector/account data. JOSHeX owns private-Mac-only work, coordination, approvals, and final review.
modelRouting.cookieKeychainDisambiguation: A visible alert mentioning cookie.codex, Codex cookies, Keychain Not Found, or a keychain cannot be found means macOS/Codex keychain work on the device. It is not Sorare cookie freshness unless the user explicitly says Sorare.
modelRouting.dailyUtilizationPolicy: Run at least two dashboard-safe Gemini specialist passes per day when quota is available: one daily digest/stale-task compression pass and one review/evaluator pass. Use scripts/gemini_specialist_pass.py --agent jaimes for JAIMES-owned passes so raw prompts and raw output are not stored. Keep Codex/Hermes as execution owner.
modelRouting.jaimesGeminiRoute: JAIMES should use Gemini first for dashboard-safe summaries, reports, digests, log summaries, stale-task compression, and model/readability evaluations. Use Gemini Flash for routine throughput and Gemini Pro for explicit deeper compact evaluation. Codex/OpenAI remains the executor for repo edits, private connectors, terminal actions, approvals, and final integration.
modelRouting.telegramDisclosurePolicy: Josh 2.0 and JAIMES must make the active model/auth route visible in Telegram. Start substantive replies with Active Model/Auth and route reason; explicit model switches must verify a fresh lane before work.
modelRouting.lastModeChangeReason: add verified fresh-lane model switching contract from JOSHeX proposal

skillPack.policy: Use matching shared skills before asking Josh for detailed procedural prompts. Skills reduce repeated prompting but do not override privacy, approval, or execution guardrails.

freshLaneSwitching.rule: Model switching means checkpoint + fresh execution lane/session + verification before substantive work; never pretend the current session mutated models.
freshLaneSwitching.explicitRequestOverride: Josh explicit model requests override the router unless unsafe or unavailable.
freshLaneSwitching.disclosure: Telegram must show Objective, Active Model/Auth, and route reason before substantive work.
freshLaneSwitching.fallbackBehavior: If requested model/provider is unavailable or unsafe for the task/privacy, state the reason and use the approved fallback lane.
